meanings.definition: To agree with someone’s opinion, statement, or decision.
examples: Most experts concur that the plan will succeed.
She concurred with the manager’s suggestion during the meeting.
I completely concur with your views on education.
The judges were concurring on the final verdict.

meanings.definition: To happen or exist at the same time together.
examples: Several problems concurred to create the crisis.
The festival concurred with the holiday season this year.
Heavy rain and strong winds were concurring throughout the night.
Their arrival concurred exactly with the start of the ceremony.
